tcd_title: MIO Trunk Clock gating Actions
tc_title: IO Trunk Clock gating_QActive deassertion
desc:
Flow *

This TC will check that late QChannels (and noise QChannels) where autonomous idle is enabled are deasserting its QActive signal.


Runtime Trunk clock gating entry flow:

Trunk clock gating entry depends on all MIO IPs reporting idleness. Entry flow will begin when both Primary and secondary traffic through MIO stops. Eventually all attached devices will enter link state L1. Controllers will de-assert their clk_req/Qactive when they are in L1. At the RC boundary accumulated Qactive will get de-asserted. RC will initiate hysteresis timer(4uS) upon de-assertion of Qactive. Once hysteresis timer expires, RC will drive out Qreq. RC will await Qaccept assertion from Qch aggregator. Once Qaccept is asserted, RC will send AIPM WP to RA to initiate trunk clock gating.


You can check Noise/Late QChannels where AIPM is enabled by checking these registers (3 instances for iMH1 / 4 instances for iMH2):

sv.socket0.imhs.resctrl.rc_mio_ew.resctrl_prvt_idle_late_regss.qch_fsm_policy.enable_auto_idle

sv.socket0.imhs.resctrl.rc_mio_ew.resctrl_prvt_idle_noise_regss.qch_fsm_policy.enable_auto_idle

QActive signals can be monitored using this PythonSV register:

sv.socket0.imhs.resctrl.rc_mio_ew.showsearch('qactive_status','f')


Example: responder (controller facing) QActive signals should be deasserted for 3 instances for iMH1 (4 instances for iMH2)  for both late and noise QChannels:


resctrl_prvt_idle_late_regs0.r_qch_status.qactive_status = 0x0

resctrl_prvt_idle_late_regs1.r_qch_status.qactive_status = 0x0

resctrl_prvt_idle_late_regs2.r_qch_status.qactive_status = 0x0

resctrl_prvt_idle_noise_regs0.r_qch_status.qactive_status = 0x0

resctrl_prvt_idle_noise_regs2.r_qch_status.qactive_status = 0x0

resctrl_prvt_idle_late_regs1.r_qch_status.qactive_status = 0x0

resctrl_prvt_idle_late_regs2.r_qch_status.qactive_status = 0x0

resctrl_prvt_idle_noise_regs0.r_qch_status.qactive_status = 0x0

resctrl_prvt_idle_noise_regs1.r_qch_status.qactive_status = 0x0

resctrl_prvt_idle_noise_regs2.r_qch_status.qactive_status = 0x0







Configuration *

- Minimal config (unpopulated IO ports) for PO.

- PCIe/CXL device supporting L1 for full coverage.

- System booting SVOS in idle scenario


Pass/Fail criteria *

Controller facing QActive signals deasserting (reporting idle) for the QChannels were auto_idle is enabled. Check both Late and Noise QChannels.


Example (Passing Case):

sv.socket0.imhs.resctrl.rc_mio_ew.showsearch('qactive_status','f')


Responder (controller facing) QActive signals should be deasserted for 3 instances for iMH1 (4 instances for iMH2)  for both late and noise QChannels:

resctrl_prvt_idle_late_regs1.r_qch_status.qactive_status = 0x0

resctrl_prvt_idle_late_regs1.r_qch_status.qactive_status = 0x0

resctrl_prvt_idle_late_regs2.r_qch_status.qactive_status = 0x0

resctrl_prvt_idle_noise_regs0.r_qch_status.qactive_status = 0x0

resctrl_prvt_idle_noise_regs1.r_qch_status.qactive_status = 0x0

resctrl_prvt_idle_noise_regs2.r_qch_status.qactive_status = 0x0


Intent Verification Strategy *

Monitor QActive signals through a PythonSV register.

HW Requirements

- Minimal config (unpopulated IO ports) for PO.

- PCIe/CXL device supporting L1 for full coverage.

- System booting SVOS in idle scenario


SW Requirements

- PythonSV

Coverage

Specify any expected coverage that is dependent on environment or configuration (not covered in the TCD's description). Also specify any coverage assessment/measurement technique that might be used, if applicable and if not stated in the TCD.

Component Interactions

Describe or illustrate the components that are involved and how they interact. This section is only needed if it provides more information than the TCD article, perhaps specific to the validation environment and framework used for this test_case.

[NWP] NWP Project Status: This TC validates the active-link TCG entry flow — QActive deassertion triggered by PCIe/CXL links entering L1. PCIe L1 is ZBB on NWP (HSD 22021155368); links never reach the idle condition needed to trigger this flow. Active-link TCG (runtime entry via link L1) is ZBB on NWP per AIPM KB §NWP Delta. Note: no-device-attached TCG (empty MIO stack) is still applicable and covered by TC 22022422984.

tcd_title: MIO Trunk Clock gating Actions
tc_title: MIO Trunk Clock gating Actions_No Devices attached in PC0
desc:
no devices are attached to IO stack to save power in PC0


HW Config

  • No IO devices (test cards) attached to the SoC (either PCIe or CXL)

SW Config

  • Package C State disable by BIOS. PackageCState = C0/C1 state

[NWP] NWP Action Item (DVP Q_STOPPED Risk): This TC triggers MIO trunk clock gating on an empty (no-device) MIO stack. On NWP, once TCG is entered, the DVP noise Q-channel enters Q_STOPPED. Unlike DMR, PkgC6 is ZBB on NWP so there is no recovery event to drive QReq back to the DVP noise QCh (DMR fix: HSD 14025929892 toggled force_qactive_enable on PkgC entry/exit). DVP will remain stuck in Q_STOPPED for the remainder of the boot once this TC runs, making ITH T2/DVP traces from that MIO stack unusable for subsequent debug.

Required verification step: Read dvp_status.qsmstatus pre-TCG (expect Q_RUN=1) and post-TCG (expect Q_STOPPED=0). Confirm with NWP DFD team whether NWP NIO RTL adds proactive QReq to noise QCh on TCG exit (if yes, Q_STOPPED may self-recover; if no, this is a known permanent condition). See AIPM KB: Combined Impact §3 and Open Actions.

[NWP ZBB — Spec Confirmed 2026-05-31] NWP spec finding (2026-05-31): Per NWP MAS, empty/unconnected stacks are handled through static stack-disable and power-gate at boot/reset, NOT by runtime AIPM trunk clock gating. BIOS detects unconnected stacks and disables them via soft straps/fuse; PrimeCode sets CMPL_STATUS=2 and skips AIPM enable. Runtime no-device TCG is NOT supported on NWP. All valid AIPM entry conditions are ZBB on NWP: PCIe L1 (HSD 22021155368), UXI L1/L0p (HSD 22021155419), PkgC6 (HSD 22021155362). ZBB: No valid positive-path AIPM TCG scenario on NWP.

tcd_title: MIO Trunk Clock gating Actions
tc_title: MIO Trunk Clock gating CXL Link in L1
desc:
Following CXL registers need to be programmed by BIOS to speed up clk_req de-assertion by the IP post L1 entry completion.

PM_LINK_HYST_TIMER.cfg_blk_n = 8 (Per port)

PM_LINK_HYST_TIMER.cfg_wait_n = 16 (Per port)

PM_PKGC_INDICATIONS.pkgc_clkreq_timer = 1

Reference: https://docs.intel.com/documents/pm_doc/src/server/DMR/PM%20Features/DMR_IO_trunk_clock_gating_support.html



Link in L1 is a pre-requisite for MIO Trunk Clock gating to happen, rather than a cross product.


This TC is going to be covered in VV, by running our content in a combination of HW/SW configs, using different types of Test Cards configured as PCIe or CXL devices, connected to both iMH0 and iMH1 and using different bifurcation modes (x16, x8, x4, x2, etc).

 

Still pending to make our content more robust to only fail when MIO TCG residency is not seen (0%) and link in L1 is happening. 

 

[NWP] NWP Project Status: CXL shares the PCIe physical layer; PCIe L1 is ZBB on NWP (HSD 22021155368). CXL links cannot enter L1 on NWP; the prerequisite idle condition for MIO trunk clock gating via active CXL links is never reached. Active-link TCG is out of scope for NWP per AIPM KB §NWP Delta.

tcd_title: MIO Trunk Clock gating Actions
tc_title: MIO Trunk Clock gating PCIe Link in L1
desc:
Link in L1 is a pre-requisite for MIO Trunk Clock gating to happen, rather than a cross product.


This TC is going to be covered in VV, by running our content in a combination of HW/SW configs, using different types of Test Cards configured as PCIe or CXL devices, connected to both iMH0 and iMH1 and using different bifurcation modes (x16, x8, x4, x2, etc).

 

Still pending to make our content more robust to only fail when MIO TCG residency is not seen (0%) and link in L1 is happening. 

 

[NWP] NWP Project Status: PCIe L1 (ASPM) is ZBB on NWP (HSD 22021155368). PCIe links cannot enter L1 on NWP; the prerequisite idle condition for MIO trunk clock gating via active PCIe links is never reached. Active-link TCG is out of scope for NWP per AIPM KB §NWP Delta.

tcd_title: MIO Trunk Clock gating Boot Time Setup
tc_title: IO Trunk Clock Gating_Late QChannel and Noise QCh enabling
desc:
Validation Scope

Verify that the late Q-Channel and noise Q-Channel autonomous-idle enable configuration is programmed correctly for the MIO trunk clock-gating infrastructure after boot. PrimeCode programs QCH_FSM_POLICY.ENABLE_AUTO_IDLE and strap defaults per MAS §9.1 during PH6. On NWP (no L1, no PkgC): trunk clock gating never fires at runtime. This TC confirms the register plumbing is correct — correct strap/policy values, no hang/MCA after setup, state preserved across re-reads. It is explicitly not a runtime entry/residency test. Reference: HSD 22021621791 — MIO TCG spec update for UXI link (RC autonomous-idle strap/policy definitions)

Preconditions

ItemRequirement
PlatformNWP validation target with AIPM boot-time setup flow enabled
FW stackPrimeCode PH6 RC workpoint programming complete; PythonSV installed
BIOSAIPM feature not force-disabled (pcode_aipm_mio_trunk_clock_gating_disable = 0)
AutomationrunPmx.py -x nwp.xml -p trunk_clkg -tM 6 (NWP config — replace dmr.xml)
Test Steps

#ActionExpected Result (PASS)Failure Indication
1Boot platform; verify no BIOS hang or MCA during PH6 RC programmingClean boot; PrimeCode RC workpoint programming completes without errorBIOS hang, MCA, or soft hang
2Read QCH_FSM_POLICY.ENABLE_AUTO_IDLE for all late QCh instances (resctrl_prvt_idle_late_regss)enable_auto_idle = 1 for enabled stacks per MAS §9.1Bit = 0 on expected stacks; or register unreadable
3Read QCH_FSM_POLICY.ENABLE_AUTO_IDLE for noise QCh instances (resctrl_prvt_idle_noise_regs0)enable_auto_idle = 1 for enabled stacks per MAS §9.1Bit = 0 on expected stacks
4Read late/noise QCh strap defaults (strap_l_autonomous_idle_enable_default, strap_n_autonomous_idle_enable_default)Strap values match PrimeCode-programmed enable mask; consistent across re-readsStrap mismatch or read instability
5Confirm disabled stacks have AIPM skipped (CMPL_STATUS=2 path, MAS §9.2)Disabled stacks: ENABLE_AUTO_IDLE = 0; PrimeCode did not program AIPM for themDisabled stack incorrectly shows AIPM enabled
Pass / Fail Criteria

PASSAll ENABLE_AUTO_IDLE bits match expected programmed values per MAS §9.1; strap defaults consistent; no hang/MCA; disabled stacks correctly skipped
FAILAny ENABLE_AUTO_IDLE mismatch; BIOS hang or MCA; disabled stack incorrectly enabled; register read instability
Health Checks — Registers & Logs

Register / LogAccessPass/Fail Criteria
Late QCh FSM policysv.socket0.imh0.resctrl.rc_mio_ew.resctrl_prvt_idle_late_regssENABLE_AUTO_IDLE bit per late QCh
Noise QCh FSM policysv.socket0.imh0.resctrl.rc_mio_ew.resctrl_prvt_idle_noise_regs0ENABLE_AUTO_IDLE bit for noise QCh
Late QCh strap defaultsv.socket0.imh0.resctrl.rc_mio_ew.strap_l_autonomous_idle_enable_defaultDefault enable mask from strap
Noise QCh strap defaultsv.socket0.imh0.resctrl.rc_mio_ew.strap_n_autonomous_idle_enable_defaultDefault enable mask from strap
AIPM fuse disablesv.sockets.imhs.fuses.punit.pcode_aipm_mio_trunk_clock_gating_disableMust be 0 for AIPM enabled
Notes & Opens

  • Automation still uses dmr.xml in HSD description → Must change to nwp.xml before execution
  • Old pass criteria reference "residency/entry" — inapplicable on NWP → Must be removed from TC description
  • Strap default values may differ between DMR and NWP RC variant → Read and compare vs MAS §9.1 expected values
  • 2026-03-31 (11 TRs): check the test steps in description match with script implementation
References

  • HSD 22021621791 — MIO TCG spec update for UXI link (RC autonomous-idle strap/policy definitions)
  • TC: 22022422990 — IO Trunk Clock Gating_Late QChannel and Noise QCh enabling
  • Script: runPmx.py -x nwp.xml -p trunk_clkg -tM 6

tcd_title: MIO Trunk Clock gating Boot Time Setup
tc_title: IO Trunk Clock gating_Auto Idle WP checkout
desc:
Validation Scope

Verify that PrimeCode correctly programs the **Auto Idle Workpoint** (`CR_CLK_WP_IDLE`, `CR_VR_WP_IDLE`) for the RC_MIO during PH6 boot initialization per MAS §9.1. The idle WP defines the clock/voltage state the RC would transition to if trunk gating ever fired — verifying it is correctly programmed ensures NWP is ready for future silicon where L1 may be enabled.


Preconditions

ItemRequirement
PlatformNWP validation target with MIO trunk clock-gating boot setup flow enabled
FW stackPrimeCode PH6 RC workpoint programming complete; PythonSV installed
Automation`runPmx.py -x nwp.xml -p trunk_clkg -tM 6` (replace `dmr.xml`)
NoteBoot/setup workpoint validation only; no runtime entry expected

Test Steps

# Action Expected Result (PASS) Failure Indication
1 Boot platform; verify clean PH6 completion No BIOS hang, MCA, or soft hang Hang or MCA during PH6
2 Read `CR_CLK_WP_ACTIVE` and `CR_CLK_WP_IDLE` from RC_MIO workpoint registers Both registers readable; idle WP ≠ active WP Unreadable or identical values
3 Read `CR_VR_WP_ACTIVE` and `CR_VR_WP_IDLE` (voltage-rail WP) Idle voltage WP programmed to lower power state than active WP WP not programmed or equal
4 Compare programmed WP values against LFCLK/FIVR RA-derived expected values per MAS §9.1 WPs match RA-sourced expected values Mismatch vs expected
5 Re-read registers after any sequencing; verify stability Values consistent across re-reads Read instability or drift

Pass / Fail Criteria

PASS

Active and idle WPs programmed, distinct, match MAS §9.1 RA-derived values; registers stable; no hang/MCA

FAIL

WPs not programmed, identical, mismatched vs RA values, or read instability



Post-Process

N/A


References

N/A

tcd_title: MIO Trunk Clock gating Boot Time Setup
tc_title: IO Trunk Clock gating_Hysterisis Timer
desc:
Disposition: Rejected — NWP
The hysteresis timer is a hardware default register. PrimeCode MAS §9.1 boot programming does not explicitly write this register. Without a PrimeCode-programmed value, there is no spec-defined expected value to verify. With no L1 or PkgC on NWP, the timer never fires and there is no runtime behavior to observe.

Why Rejected (Not Just Descoped)

TC PrimeCode Programs It? Status Reason
22022422990 Late/Noise QCh enabling ✅ Yes — ENABLE_AUTO_IDLE Open Programmed value verifiable
22022422992 Auto Idle WP checkout ✅ Yes — CR_CLK_WP_IDLE/ACTIVE Open Programmed value verifiable
22022422994 Hysteresis Timer ❌ No — hardware default Rejected No programmed value; timer never fires on NWP
22022423000 RC Capability0 checkout ✅ Yes — hardware discovery register Open Capability register always meaningful

References

  • NWP PM MAS §9.1 — RC boot programming list (hysteresis timer not included)
  • RC TRM HAS — Hysteresis timer register definition

tcd_title: MIO Trunk Clock gating Boot Time Setup
tc_title: IO Trunk Clock gating_RC Capability0 reg checkout
desc:
Validation Scope

Verify that **RC Capability0** (`resctrl_prv_capability0`) is populated with the correct values for the NWP RC_MIO Gen4 lnpv variant: `NUM_QCH_LATE=9`, `NUM_QCH_NOISE=9`, `NUM_IDLE_WORKPOINTS=2`. This is a **discovery/sanity checkout** — confirming the RC IP instance matches the expected NWP configuration and that PrimeCode boot programming is targeting the correct variant.


Preconditions

ItemRequirement
PlatformNWP validation target with RC_MIO accessible post-boot
FW stackPrimeCode PH6 RC programming complete; PythonSV installed
Automation`runPmx.py -x nwp.xml -p trunk_clkg -tM 6` (replace `dmr.xml`)
NoteRegister capability/discovery validation only

Test Steps

# Action Expected Result (PASS) Failure Indication
1 Boot platform; verify clean PH6 completion No BIOS hang, MCA, or soft hang Hang or MCA during PH6
2 Read `resctrl_prv_capability0` register Register readable; returns non-zero Unreadable or zero
3 Verify `NUM_QCH_LATE` field = 9 (NWP Gen4 lnpv) `NUM_QCH_LATE` = 9 Wrong value (e.g., 4 for earlier variant)
4 Verify `NUM_QCH_NOISE` field = 9 `NUM_QCH_NOISE` = 9 Wrong value
5 Verify `NUM_IDLE_WORKPOINTS` field = 2 (active WP + idle WP) `NUM_IDLE_WORKPOINTS` = 2 Wrong value
6 Re-read for stability; check no PM config errors in logs Consistent values across re-reads; no errors Value instability or PM error

Pass / Fail Criteria

PASS

All three fields (`NUM_QCH_LATE`=9, `NUM_QCH_NOISE`=9, `NUM_IDLE_WORKPOINTS`=2) match NWP Gen4 lnpv spec; registers stable; no hang/MCA

FAIL

Any field mismatch; register unreadable; hang/MCA; PM config error



Post-Process

N/A


References

N/A

tcd_title: MIO Trunk Clock gating Cross products
tc_title: IO Trunk Clock gating x ADR
desc:
https://docs.intel.com/documents/pm_doc/src/server/DMR/PM%20Features/DMR_IO_trunk_clock_gating_support.html#adr

Flow *

ADR:

Cross between ADR and Trunk gating caused by AIPM is a valid scenario. PrimeCode will initiate PM unwind flow once it receives ADR notification from S3M. Part of its PM unwind flow, PrimeCode will program RC’s Qch register RESCTRL_QCH_LATE_CR_QCH_FSM_POLICY and RESCTRL_QCH_NOISE_CR_QCH_FSM_POLICY(up to 4 instances each) to disable AIPM. RC will trigger trunk clock ungating when AIPM gets disabled.-


QChannel late QCH_FSM_POLICY can be checked by this PythonSV command:

sv.socket0.imhs.resctrl.rc_mio_ew.resctrl_prvt_idle_late_regss.qch_fsm_policy.enable_auto_idle


QChannel noise QCH_FSM_POLICY can be checked by this PythonSV command:

sv.socket0.imhs.resctrl.rc_mio_ew.resctrl_prvt_idle_noise_regss.qch_fsm_policy.enable_auto_idle


In a VV config with just AIPM enabled (PC6 disabled, in order to increase residency in AIPM), initiate ADR. No BIOS hangs, MCAs or soft hangs are expected. SOC configs, in terms of enabled IPs, should be the same prior and after each ADR flow.

Configuration *

How does the platform need to be configured to obtain the desired coverage? For example: fuses, bios knobs, pre-test scripts, kernel/OS config. In the case of BIOS knobs, list all that impact this coverage, including both custom and default.

Pass/Fail criteria *

Examples: register values to look for, what content output to look for on the screen, key words or sections in the log.

Intent Verification Strategy *

How will TC logic be checked out prior to testing on silicon (e.g. on N-1 hardware, in Simics, etc.)? If not possible, explain why (e.g. new feature not present in N-1 and not modelled in pre-si environment).

HW Requirements

What besides silicon is required for execution of this test? For example: logic analyzer, a thermal head, an Intel test card or IHV device, etc.

SW Requirements

What software besides actual test content is needed for execution of this test? For example: special version of operating system, drivers, firmware, or harassers (to run with the main test content), software tool(s).

Coverage

tcd_title: MIO Trunk Clock gating Cross products
tc_title: IO Trunk Clock gating x PC6
desc:
https://docs.intel.com/documents/pm_doc/src/server/DMR/PM%20Features/DMR_IO_trunk_clock_gating_support.html#pkgc

"

pkgC:

When entering PkgC, if an AIPM workpoint change has already started in the workpoint sequencer, then the AIPM change must complete before attempting the PkgC entry. If PkgC starts first, then the AIPM change can be dropped.

If the RC autonomously enters idle and later the SoC detects PkgC entry conditions, RC is expected to transition from AIPM WP to pkgC WP without first popping back up to trunk ungated state.

"


The goal for this TC is to check cross functionality between flows MIO Trunk Clock gating and PC6 in MIO. 


Strategy would be to have both MIO Trunk Clock gating and PC6 enabled at the same time. One way to make sure both flows are happening is to check PMON registers for MIO RC and program one event for AIPM and another event for PC6 and make sure that PMON counters are increasing (both events are happening).

 

The purpose of this TC is to make sure the Performance Debug counters for Autonomous entry and PC6 entry are both incrementing.

This validation can be achieved by programming one of the 6 perfdbg registers in the RC to the event of Autonomous Entry for the interested QChannel, and another perfdbg register for PC6 event in the RC for MIO


In [72]:sv.socket0.imh0.uncore.rc.rc0.resctrl_pblc_perfdbg_regs.perfdbg_cntr_ctrl

Out[72]:

perfdbg_cntr_ctrl[0]=0x0

perfdbg_cntr_ctrl[1]=0x0

perfdbg_cntr_ctrl[2]=0x0

perfdbg_cntr_ctrl[3]=0x0

perfdbg_cntr_ctrl[4]=0x0

perfdbg_cntr_ctrl[5]=0x0


Once program, we need to check the corresponding perfdbg_data registers (same instance) and make sure is incrementing as the event is happening:

 

                                                                                         

Name

 

 

RESCTRL_CR_PERFDBG_CNTR_CTRL[0]

Size

 

 

64

Address Offset

 

 

0xf300

SAI Group

 

 

RESCTRL_PBLC_UARCH_PG

Description

 

 

Performance  Debug Counter Configuration Register

Bits

Access

Default

Description

7:0

RW/V

0x0

EVENT_SELECT

  

Minimum  field to select which of the available events should be recorded in the  corresponding counter. Additional bits in the control register may also be  required to select from the available events. e.g.  0x0 == Count Nothing  0x1 == Count Local Clockticks. Refer to the Resource Controller Technical  Reference Manual for the full list of supported values.

  

write_is_allowed:  anytime

15:8

RW/V

0x0

UMASK

  

IP  Specific Encoding Space. Resource Controller uses this as an extension of  EVENT_SELECT, such as selecting a particular instance of Q-Channel FSM, or  particular FSM state. Refer to the Resource Controller Technical Reference  Manual for the full list of supported values.

  

write_is_allowed:  anytime

17:17

RW/1S/V

0x0

RST

  

When  set to 1, the corresponding counter will be cleared to 0. All counter reset  controls have lower precedence than counter freeze controls. Counters will  not be reset while frozen. Hardware will clear this after 1 cycle

  

write_is_allowed:  anytime

18:18

RW/V

0x0

EDGE_DETECT

  

When  set to 1, rather than measuring the event in each cycle it is active, the  corresponding counter will increment when a 0 to 1 transition  (i.e. rising edge) is detected. When 0, the counter will increment in  each cycle that the event is asserted.

  

write_is_allowed:  anytime

20:20

RW/V

0x0

RSVD_FRZ_ON_OVERFLOW

  

Reserved  for future feature enabling. When an overflow is detected from this register,  a PMON overflow message is sent to the global control. This bit will tell the  global control whether it should assert the global freeze for all counters in  the same domain.

  

write_is_allowed:  anytime

23:23

RW/V

0x0

RSVD_INVERT

  

Usage  - should not be used in current generation, usage reserved for future  generation. Invert comparison against Threshold. 0 - comparison will be - is  event increment greater-than-or-equal to threshold? 1 - comparison is  inverted - is event increment less-than threshold?

  

                 e.g. for a 64-entry queue, if SW wanted to know how many cycles the  queue had fewer than 4 entries, SW should set the threshold to 4 and set the  invert bit to 1.
                    

  

write_is_allowed:  never

31:24

RW/V

0x0

RSVD_THRESHOLD

  

Usage  - should not be used in current generation, usage reserved for future  generation. Threshold is used, along with the invert bit, to compare against  the incoming increment value that will be added to the counter. For events  that increment by more than 1 per cycle, if the threshold is set to a value  greater than 1, the data register will accumulate instances in which the  event increment is >= threshold.

  

write_is_allowed:  never

4.3.0.7.2.2 PERFDBG_DATA

                                               

Name

 

 

RESCTRL_CR_PERFDBG_DATA[0]

Size

 

 

64

Address Offset

 

 

0xf400

SAI Group

 

 

RESCTRL_PBLC_UARCH_PG

Description

 

 

Holds  the current value of the performance debug counter. There is one instance of  this register per PERFDBG counter resource

Bits

Access

Default

Description

31:0

RW/V

0x0

DATA

  

Holds  the current value of the performance debug counter. This CR can be written if  such a usage model is desired. Any counter increment event will be squashed  if it occurs on the same cycle as a CR write

  

write_is_allowed:  anytime

 

From<https://docs.intel.com/documents/sysip_pm/resource_controller/TRM/TRM.html#perfdbg_spare>


Program the event as below:


                

Event

perfdbg_cntr_ctrl.event_select encoding

perfdbg_cntr_ctrl.umask encoding

Description

Q-Channel  FSM

8’h40  .. 8’h80

umask[7:5]  = Qch_Mode

  

umask[2:0]  = Qch_FSM_State

event_select  mapping:

  

0x5F..0x40  - Noise Q-Channel[31:0]

  

0x7F..0x60  - Late Q-Channel[31:0]

  

0x80  - Internal Telemetry Q-Channel

  

 

  

Qch_Mode  encodings (umask[7:5]):

  

3’d0  - FSM_STATE - emits a perfdbg increment when the current FSM state matches  umask[2:0]

  

3’d1  - FSM_STATE_ABORT - emits a perfdbg increment when the FSM state transitons  out of Qch_FSM_State through an “abort” arc. The “abort” arcs include:  HYSTERESIS->IDLE, Q_ENTER->Q_EXIT, Q_GLOBAL_ENTER->RESOURCE_ACTIVE,  Q_GLOBAL_IDLE->RESOURCE_ACTIVE.

  

3’d2  - QDENY - generates a perfdbg increment on every cycle i_qch_deny is 1.

  

3’d3 - AUTONOMOUS_ENTRY - emit  a perfdbg increment whenever the FSM state transitions into Q_ENTER state on  a commit to autonomous entry

  

3’d4 - PKGC_ENTRY - emit a  perfdbg increment whenever the FSM state transitions into Q_ENTER state on an  attempt to enter package-C

  

 

  

Qch_FSM_State  encodings (umask[2:0]):

  

3’h0  - IDLE

  

3’h1  - HYSTERESIS

  

3’h2  - Q_ENTER

  

3’h3  - RESOURCE_IDLE

  

3’h4  - Q_GLOBAL_ENTER

  

3’h5  - Q_GLOBAL_IDLE

  

3’h6  - RESOURCE_ACTIVE

  

3’h7  - Q_EXIT

  

 

  

Note:  Qch_FSM_State (umask[2:0]) is only considered for the subset of Qch_Mode  (umask[7:5]) encodings that specifically mention the qualification


OPEN: NOISE and LATE Qchannel mapping is needed in order to execute this TC.

[NWP] NWP Project Status: PkgC6 is fused off on NWP (FUSE_PKG_C_STATE=0). ZBB HSD: 22021155362. The TCG x PkgC6 cross-product from DMR HAS section does not apply to NWP. MC6 is the deepest idle state on NWP.

tcd_title: MIO Trunk Clock gating Cross products
tc_title: IO Trunk Clock gating x Warm Reset
desc:
Validation Scope

Validate IO trunk clock gating behavior across Warm Reset when PrimeCode receives a warm reset notification and executes PM unwind. As part of unwind, RC QCH policies are programmed to disable AIPM and trigger trunk clock ungating. The test verifies reset stability and configuration consistency across repeated warm reset stress cycles (no BIOS hang, no MCA, no soft hang).

Preconditions

ItemRequirement
PlatformNWP/DMR silicon with PrimeCode PM unwind support for Warm Reset.
Feature configurationVV config with AIPM enabled and PC6 disabled (to increase AIPM residency).
ToolingPythonSV access for RC QCH register checks and serial log collection.
Reference commandsv.socket0.imhs.resctrl.rc_mio_ew.resctrl_prvt_idle_late_regss.qch_fsm_policy.enable_auto_idle
sv.socket0.imhs.resctrl.rc_mio_ew.resctrl_prvt_idle_noise_regss.qch_fsm_policy.enable_auto_idle
Test Steps

#ActionExpected Result (PASS)Failure Indication
1Boot SUT with target VV configuration (AIPM enabled, PC6 disabled).System boots cleanly and PM services initialize.Boot failure, missing PM init, or unexpected fatal errors.
2Establish baseline RC QCH policy values before reset.Baseline values are readable and consistent.Registers unreadable, inconsistent, or invalid.
3Trigger Warm Reset and allow PrimeCode PM unwind to execute.PrimeCode processes warm reset notification and applies QCH policy changes to disable AIPM.Missing unwind behavior or no policy transition.
4Read late/noise QCH policy registers post-reset.Policy reflects expected disable-auto-idle behavior after warm reset unwind.Policy remains unchanged or reflects incorrect state.
5Run warm reset stress (multiple iterations).No BIOS hang, no MCA, no soft hang across iterations.Any hang, MCA, watchdog, or reset instability.
6Compare enabled SOC/IP configuration before vs after resets.Enabled IP topology/configuration remains consistent.Unexpected IP enablement delta or configuration drift.
Health Check — Registers & Logs to Collect

Register / LogAccessPass/Fail Criteria
QCH late policysv.socket0.imhs.resctrl.rc_mio_ew.resctrl_prvt_idle_late_regss.qch_fsm_policy.enable_auto_idlePASS: value shows expected post-unwind state; FAIL: unchanged/invalid.
QCH noise policysv.socket0.imhs.resctrl.rc_mio_ew.resctrl_prvt_idle_noise_regss.qch_fsm_policy.enable_auto_idlePASS: value shows expected post-unwind state; FAIL: unchanged/invalid.
Serial log / reset logPlatform serial console and reset execution logsPASS: no BIOS hang/MCA/soft hang signatures; FAIL: any fatal/reset instability signature.
Post-Process

Declare PASS only if all reset iterations complete without hang/MCA and RC QCH policy registers match expected warm-reset unwind behavior while platform configuration remains stable pre/post reset. Declare FAIL on any reset instability, policy mismatch, or configuration drift.

Reference

  • DMR IO trunk clock gating support - Warm Reset
  • PrimeCode PM unwind flow requirements for Warm Reset notification handling.

[Traceability] Previous description snapshot is preserved in test_case.notes (migrated 2026-06-02 16:35:45). API comment write is unsupported for server.test_case on this endpoint.

tcd_title: MIO Trunk Clock gating Cross products
tc_title: MIO Trunk Clock Gating x Hot Plug / Hot Eject
desc:
https://docs.intel.com/documents/pm_doc/src/server/DMR/PM%20Features/DMR_IO_trunk_clock_gating_support.html#hot-plug

https://docs.intel.com/documents/pm_doc/src/server/DMR/PM%20Features/DMR_IO_trunk_clock_gating_support.html#hot-eject


Flow *

Hot Plug:

For the hotplug event CXPSMB IP will trigger OS/Driver interrupt. This IP runs on always on 100MHz clock. OS/Driver will send notification to controller to initiate power on. This will result in ASF causing trunk ungating if Trunk was gated.

Hot Eject:

User will press alert button that will result in a interrupt for OS/Driver. OS will send notification to controller to initiate polling. This communication will result in ASF causing trunk ungating if trunk was already gated due to idleness.

Alternatively if user pulls card without notifying driver/OS, Presence detect on the slot will go from 1 to 0. This causes SMALERT# assertion. CXPSMB IP will send sideband transaction to all root ports causing wake to trunk ungating.


Configuration *

How does the platform need to be configured to obtain the desired coverage? For example: fuses, bios knobs, pre-test scripts, kernel/OS config. In the case of BIOS knobs, list all that impact this coverage, including both custom and default.

Pass/Fail criteria *

Examples: register values to look for, what content output to look for on the screen, key words or sections in the log.

Intent Verification Strategy *

How will TC logic be checked out prior to testing on silicon (e.g. on N-1 hardware, in Simics, etc.)? If not possible, explain why (e.g. new feature not present in N-1 and not modelled in pre-si environment).

HW Requirements

What besides silicon is required for execution of this test? For example: logic analyzer, a thermal head, an Intel test card or IHV device, etc.

SW Requirements

What software besides actual test content is needed for execution of this test? For example: special version of operating system, drivers, firmware, or harassers (to run with the main test content), software tool(s).

Coverage

Specify any expected coverage that is dependent on environment or configuration (not covered in the TCD's description). Also specify any coverage assessment/measurement technique that might be used, if applicable and if not stated in the TCD.

Component Interactions

Describe or illustrate the components that are involved and how they interact. This section is only needed if it provides more information than the TCD article, perhaps specific to the validation environment and framework used for this test_case.

[NWP ZBB — Spec Confirmed 2026-05-31] NWP spec finding (2026-05-31): This TC requires trunk clock gating to have been entered as a precondition (device inserted into a previously gated stack). Per NWP MAS, there is no valid AIPM TCG entry path on NWP: PCIe L1 ZBB (HSD 22021155368), UXI L1/L0p ZBB (HSD 22021155419), PkgC6 ZBB (HSD 22021155362), empty stacks statically disabled at boot (not AIPM-gated). Without a reachable gated state, hot plug/eject ungate scenario is not testable. ZBB: Entry precondition unreachable on NWP.

tcd_title: MIO Trunk Clock gating Cross products
tc_title: MIO Trunk Clock gating x IO Stack Disable
desc:
Validation Scope

https://docs.intel.com/documents/pm_doc/src/server/DMR/PM%20Features/DMR_IO_trunk_clock_gating_support.html#io-stack-disable Flow * IO stack disable: MIO stack can be disabled using fuse and soft straps . When MIO stack is disabled it may or may not be powered off depending up on which FIVR is feeding it. PrimeCode will unlock MIO PLL during Phase 6 of reset. IPs within the MIO stack will not be pulled out of reset. clk_req/Qactive from these IPs will be isolated at ISA and Qch aggregator based on IP disable from reset widget. FSA will terminate all sideband transaction targeted for MIO IPs as Side_POK will not be asserted by IP. FSA will continue to assert Qactive in case a transaction was targeted for MIO stack even though it terminated it. To avoid any unwanted trunk ungating while IO stack is disabled, PrimeCode will qualify stack disable before enabling AIPM for MIO . It will only e

PlatformNWP validation target with Autonomous Idle PM flow enabled as applicable.

tcd_title: MIO Trunk Clock gating Cross products
tc_title: MIO Trunk Clock gating x Seamless patching
desc:
https://docs.intel.com/documents/pm_doc/src/server/DMR/PM%20Features/DMR_IO_trunk_clock_gating_support.html#seamless-patching

Flow *

Seamless Patching:

For seamless patching S3M sends patch load request to Aventine PHY Buttress through sideband. In case such request comes through while trunk is gated, FSA will drive Qactive due to sideband transaction. This Qactive assertion will cause RC to unwind trunk gating.


Configuration *

[NWP ZBB — Spec Confirmed 2026-05-31] NWP spec finding (2026-05-31): This TC requires trunk clock gating to be active when the seamless patch load request arrives (FSA drives QActive to unwind TCG). Per NWP MAS, there is no valid AIPM TCG entry path on NWP: PCIe L1 ZBB (HSD 22021155368), UXI L1/L0p ZBB (HSD 22021155419), PkgC6 ZBB (HSD 22021155362), empty stacks statically disabled at boot (not AIPM-gated). ZBB: TCG entry precondition unreachable on NWP.

tcd_title: MIO Trunk Clock gating Cross products
tc_title: MIO Trunk Clock gating x Surprise Reset
desc:
Validation Scope

https://docs.intel.com/documents/pm_doc/src/server/DMR/PM%20Features/DMR_IO_trunk_clock_gating_support.html#surprise-reset Flow * Surprise Reset: During Surprise reset PrimeCode actions can not be guaranteed. Hence rather than relying on PrimeCode to do unwind actions, we will rely on hardware to cause trunk ungating. As shown in the diagram above, HWRS will communicate surprise reset to Reset widget using reset but. Reset Widget will drive Disable_Auto_PM wire to all ISA. ISA will connect this wire in its Qch interface to cause Qactive assertion whenever Disable_Auto_PM is asserted. Qactive assertion will cause RC to ungate trunk clock. As we need to implement HWRS based unwind during surprise warm reset, we will leverage same mechanism for unwind during warm reset. No PrimeCode PM unwind action is required to disable AIPM during warm reset. Configuration * How does the platform need to

event_select mapping:

0x5F..0x40 - Noise Q-Channel[31:0]

0x7F..0x60 - Late Q-Channel[31:0]

0x80 - Internal Telemetry Q-Channel

 

Qch_Mode encodings (umask[7:5]):

3’d0 - FSM_STATE - emits a perfdbg increment when the current FSM state matches umask[2:0]

3’d1 - FSM_STATE_ABORT - emits a perfdbg increment when the FSM state transitons out of Qch_FSM_State through an “abort” arc. The “abort” arcs include: HYSTERESIS->IDLE, Q_ENTER->Q_EXIT, Q_GLOBAL_ENTER->RESOURCE_ACTIVE, Q_GLOBAL_IDLE->RESOURCE_ACTIVE.

3’d2 - QDENY - generates a perfdbg increment on every cycle i_qch_deny is 1.

3’d3 - AUTONOMOUS_ENTRY - emit a perfdbg increment whenever the FSM state transitions into Q_ENTER state on a commit to autonomous entry

3’d4 - PKGC_ENTRY - emit a perfdbg increment whenever the FSM state transitions into Q_ENTER state on an attempt to enter package-C

 

Qch_FSM_State encodings (umask[2:0]):

3’h0 - IDLE

3’h1 - HYSTERESIS

3’h2 - Q_ENTER

3’h3 - RESOURCE_IDLE

3’h4 - Q_GLOBAL_ENTER

3’h5 - Q_GLOBAL_IDLE

3’h6 - RESOURCE_ACTIVE

3’h7 - Q_EXIT

 

Note: Qch_FSM_State (umask[2:0]) is only considered for the subset of Qch_Mode (umask[7:5]) encodings that specifically mention the qualification

[NWP ZBB — Spec Confirmed 2026-05-31] NWP spec finding (2026-05-31): This TC measures autonomous AIPM TCG entry events via RC perfdbg_cntr_ctrl. Per NWP MAS, there is no valid autonomous TCG entry on NWP: PCIe L1 ZBB (HSD 22021155368), UXI L1/L0p ZBB (HSD 22021155419), PkgC6 ZBB (HSD 22021155362), empty stacks statically disabled (no AIPM). Counter will never increment. ZBB: No autonomous entry event possible on NWP.

tcd_title: MIO Trunk Clock gating Entry / Residency
tc_title: MIO Trunk Clock gating Spurious Wakes
desc:
Flow *

AR From External Test Plan reviews: To verify no spurious wake up (ex, temperature update, GPSB accesses, telemetry) occurs from trunk clock gating.  Need to check on the visibility of these spurious events. 

Configuration *

How does the platform need to be configured to obtain the desired coverage? For example: fuses, bios knobs, pre-test scripts, kernel/OS config. In the case of BIOS knobs, list all that impact this coverage, including both custom and default.

Pass/Fail criteria *

Examples: register values to look for, what content output to look for on the screen, key words or sections in the log.

Intent Verification Strategy *

How will TC logic be checked out prior to testing on silicon (e.g. on N-1 hardware, in Simics, etc.)? If not possible, explain why (e.g. new feature not present in N-1 and not modelled in pre-si environment).

HW Requirements

What besides silicon is required for execution of this test? For example: logic analyzer, a thermal head, an Intel test card or IHV device, etc.

SW Requirements

What software besides actual test content is needed for execution of this test? For example: special version of operating system, drivers, firmware, or harassers (to run with the main test content), software tool(s).

Coverage

Specify any expected coverage that is dependent on environment or configuration (not covered in the TCD's description). Also specify any coverage assessment/measurement technique that might be used, if applicable and if not stated in the TCD.

Component Interactions

Describe or illustrate the components that are involved and how they interact. This section is only needed if it provides more information than the TCD article, perhaps specific to the validation environment and framework used for this test_case.

[NWP ZBB — Spec Confirmed 2026-05-31] NWP spec finding (2026-05-31): This TC verifies no spurious TCG exit from temperature updates, GPSB accesses, telemetry -- requires trunk clock gating to be active. Per NWP MAS, there is no valid AIPM TCG entry path on NWP: PCIe L1 ZBB (HSD 22021155368), UXI L1/L0p ZBB (HSD 22021155419), PkgC6 ZBB (HSD 22021155362), empty stacks statically disabled (not AIPM-gated). ZBB: No gated state to exit from on NWP.

tcd_title: MC Shallow Self Refresh Actions
tc_title: MC Shallow Self Refresh_PActive deassertion
desc: No Memory PM on NWP: No APD/PPD/LPM/SSR/SR per NWP PM MAS. MC Shallow Self-Refresh requires DRAM idle autonomous power-down which is ZBB on NWP (HSD 22021155412). Parent TCD already rejected. [val_agent] Rejected for NWP 2026-05-31
